description: Close the pre-existing hex-intf-specific leaks in hex-cli/assets/ (skills, agents, hooks, context-templates, ci, and the scaffold parallel tree), then add a hex doctor guardrail that fails CI on any future regression. Follow-up to the 2026-04-11 cleanup that deleted hex-publish-module, hex-spacetime, and hex-dev-rebuild from the embed set.

phases.description: Audit the three remaining suspect skills in hex-cli/assets/skills/ for hex-intf markers and either genericize in place or delete-and-preserve-in-.claude if fundamentally project-specific.
phases.name: Audit and fix skills leaks

tasks.description: Grep for hex-nexus, hex-cli, hex-core, spacetime-modules markers. Categorize each hit as (a) generic example that can stay, (b) hex-intf-specific that must be replaced with language-agnostic wording, or (c) fundamentally project-specific that requires delete-and-preserve-in-.claude.
tasks.name: Audit hex-adr-create.md for hex-nexus references

tasks.description: Same marker grep as P1.1. This skill describes architecture analysis — references to hex-nexus/hex-cli as the 'example project' must be removed or replaced with <your-project>.
tasks.name: Audit hex-analyze-arch/SKILL.md for crate references

tasks.description: This skill describes the feature-development workflow. References to the hex-intf Rust workspace layout (6 crates) must be abstracted to language-agnostic phase descriptions. The 7-phase lifecycle itself is generic and should stay.
tasks.name: Audit hex-feature-dev/SKILL.md for workspace specifics

tasks.description: For each audit finding: if generic-with-leaks, edit in place to strip hex-intf markers; if project-specific, rm from hex-cli/assets/skills/ and verify the file still exists under .claude/skills/ (preserve hex-intf's own use). cargo check -p hex-cli must stay green throughout.
tasks.name: Apply fixes from P1.1-P1.3 (edit or delete)

phases.description: Apply the same marker-audit-then-fix approach to the non-skills assets. Each file either gets genericized in place or deleted from hex-cli/assets/ with a preservation check against hex-intf's live copy.
phases.name: Audit and fix agents, hooks, context-templates, ci

tasks.description: The agent YAML references cargo build -p hex-nexus, hex-core, hex-cli by name in its review checklist. Replace with language-agnostic build commands (e.g. 'the project build command' or a variable the agent resolves from project config).
tasks.name: Fix adversarial-reviewer.yml crate references

tasks.description: Both hooks reference hex-nexus/src/ paths in their descriptions or example violations. Rewrite to use generic adapter-boundary language.
tasks.name: Fix hex-adr-lifecycle.yml and hex-no-rest-state-mutation.yml hook paths

tasks.description: read.md, grep.md, edit.md each include examples referencing hex-nexus or hex-cli paths. Replace with generic src/ or <adapter>/ examples that translate to any language.
tasks.name: Fix context-templates/tools/*.md tool guidance

tasks.description: hexflo-global.md has hardcoded /Volumes/ExtendedStorage/hex-intf paths. session-memory.md has cargo build -p hex-nexus specific commands. Both need to be rewritten to describe the concept generically without referencing hex's own deployment.
tasks.name: Fix context-templates/services/{hexflo-global,session-memory}.md

tasks.description: The CI template references /Volumes/ and hex-intf layout. Decide: (a) genericize to a language-agnostic CI template, or (b) move to hex-cli/assets/ci/examples/rust-workspace.yml as a named example and ship an empty-by-default ci/ directory. Option (b) is cleaner — a CI template that leaks repo-specific paths is worse than no template.
tasks.name: Fix or remove ci/hex-ci.yml

phases.description: hex-cli/assets/scaffold/.claude/ is a second embed subtree used by hex init / hex scaffold. It currently duplicates much of hex-cli/assets/skills/ with its own independent drift. Establish a single source of truth or a documented intentional divergence.
phases.name: Reconcile scaffold parallel tree

tasks.description: Read hex-cli/src/commands/init.rs and any scaffold command to determine how hex-cli/assets/scaffold/ is consumed vs hex-cli/assets/skills/. Are both consumed? Is one authoritative? Document the answer in a comment at the top of init.rs.
tasks.name: Read hex-cli init and scaffold command handlers

tasks.description: Based on P3.1 findings, either (a) delete the scaffold duplicate subtree and have init read from hex-cli/assets/skills/, (b) keep both but add a cargo build-time check that asserts they're byte-identical, or (c) document what intentionally differs and add a test. Preferred: (a) if feasible.
tasks.name: Decide reconciliation strategy and execute

phases.description: Implement the CI-enforceable check described in ADR-2604111142 §Decision.3. After P1-P3 have cleaned the current state, this check prevents any future edit from re-introducing the pollution.
phases.name: Add hex doctor embedded-assets-generic guardrail

tasks.description: In hex-cli/src/commands/doctor.rs (or the checks module it delegates to), add a new check that walks Assets::iter() and greps every embedded file for the marker regex: /Volumes/|hex-intf|hex-(nexus|cli|core|agent|parser|desktop)(/src/|-[a-z]+|\s)|spacetime-modules/|hexflo-coordination|agent-registry|inference-gateway|secret-grant|rl-engine|chat-relay|neural-lab. Exception: the literal hex CLI commands (hex analyze, hex plan execute, hex init, etc.) are allowed — those are the user-facing interface.
tasks.name: Add embedded-assets-generic check to hex doctor

tasks.description: Update hex-cli/src/commands/ci.rs so that `hex ci` runs the embedded-assets-generic check in its strict-mode sweep. Any marker hit fails the CI run with a clear message pointing at file:line and the matched marker.
tasks.name: Wire the check into hex ci strict mode

tasks.description: Add a unit test in hex-cli/src/commands/doctor.rs that feeds a synthetic in-memory 'embedded asset' containing /Volumes/ExtendedStorage and asserts the check reports it as a failure with the expected file:marker message. Also a positive test with a clean synthetic asset that must pass.
tasks.name: Add regression test with poison fixture

tasks.description: Add a short section to CLAUDE.md under the 'hex-cli/assets — Embedded Templates' block explaining the generic-only rule, the marker list, and how to add exceptions (via an allowlist file that the check reads — design TBD, can land in a follow-up). Link back to ADR-2604111142.
tasks.name: Document the guardrail in CLAUDE.md
